title: dag: Define StateType enum and StateNode struct
description: Implement core StateType enum (Pass, Task, Wait, Choice, Parallel, Map, Succeed, Fail) and StateNode struct per ADR-002

resolved.question: StateConfig enum variants?
resolved.answer: All AWS Step Functions state config variants per ADR-004 parity spec

assumptions.assumption: StateConfig will be defined in same crate using strum for derives
assumptions.risk_if_wrong: May need to add strum to wtf-core dependencies

ubiquitous: THE SYSTEM SHALL define all 8 StateType variants matching AWS Step Functions
THE SYSTEM SHALL provide serde serialization for all state types

event_driven.trigger: WHEN a workflow is loaded from storage
event_driven.shall: THE SYSTEM SHALL deserialize StateType and StateNode correctly

unwanted.condition: IF invalid state type is deserialized
unwanted.shall_not: THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT panic or produce undefined behavior
unwanted.because: Invalid data from storage should be handled gracefully

postconditions: StateNode has id: u32, name: String, state_type: StateType, config: StateConfig fields
All types derive Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize
invariants: StateNode.id is unique within a WorkflowGraph

questions: Are there existing strum uses in the codebase?

phase_1: Define StateConfig enum with Step Functions variants
phase_2: Verify all types compile with correct derives

similar_implementations: See petgraph graph types for weight/node patterns

title: dag: Define Connection struct and WorkflowGraph type alias
description: Implement Connection struct with source_port/target_port and WorkflowGraph = DiGraph<StateNode, Connection> type alias

ubiquitous: THE SYSTEM SHALL use Connection to represent directed edges in the workflow graph
THE SYSTEM SHALL provide port-based edge filtering capability

event_driven.shall: THE SYSTEM SHALL filter edges by port name

unwanted.condition: IF an edge has empty port names
unwanted.shall_not: THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT allow such edges to be created
unwanted.because: Empty ports would cause undefined traversal behavior

postconditions: Connection struct has source_port: String and target_port: String
WorkflowGraph is a type alias for DiGraph<StateNode, Connection>
invariants: Port names match defined patterns: 'main', 'true', 'false', or /^branch_\d+$/

phase_1: Define Connection struct with source_port and target_port

title: dag: Implement WorkflowDag wrapper with execution_order and ready_nodes
description: Implement WorkflowDag wrapper struct with entry_point, and methods: execution_order() using toposort, ready_nodes() for completed predecessor detection

resolved.question: What happens if the graph has cycles?
resolved.answer: toposort returns Err on cycles, which should be converted to a proper error type

assumptions.assumption: WorkflowDag owns the graph and entry_point
assumptions.validation_method: ADR-002 shows graph and entry_point as fields

ubiquitous: THE SYSTEM SHALL return nodes in topologically sorted order for execution
THE SYSTEM SHALL return only nodes whose predecessors are all completed

event_driven.shall: THE SYSTEM SHALL return Vec<NodeIndex> in topological order
THE SYSTEM SHALL return nodes ready to execute given completed set

unwanted.condition: IF the graph contains a cycle
unwanted.shall_not: THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT panic in execution_order, but return an error
unwanted.because: Cyclic workflows should be a recoverable validation error

preconditions: completed set contains NodeIndex values that exist in the graph
postconditions: execution_order returns all node indices in valid topological order
ready_nodes returns only nodes with all predecessors in completed set
Both methods return Result types for fallible operations
invariants: WorkflowDag.graph is always a valid DiGraph
entry_point always points to a valid node

questions: How to handle toposort error type conversion?

phase_1: Create WorkflowDag struct with graph and entry_point fields
Handle CycleError by converting to custom error type
phase_2: Write comprehensive tests for both methods
Verify error handling for cyclic graphs

similar_implementations: See petgraph toposort examples for error handling patterns

title: dag: Implement edges_from and parallel_branches methods
description: Implement edges_from() for port-filtered edge traversal and parallel_branches() for detecting fan-out from Parallel state nodes

resolved.question: How to identify parallel branch edges?
resolved.answer: Parallel state edges use 'branch_N' port naming where N is branch index

ubiquitous: THE SYSTEM SHALL filter edges by source_port when traversing
THE SYSTEM SHALL group parallel branches by branch identifier

event_driven.shall: THE SYSTEM SHALL return only edges where weight.source_port equals port
THE SYSTEM SHALL return Vec<Vec<NodeIndex>> grouped by branch

unwanted.condition: IF parallel_branches is called on a non-Parallel node
unwanted.shall_not: THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT panic but may return empty result
unwanted.because: Type safety at call site cannot be enforced, defensive programming required

preconditions: node parameter must be a valid NodeIndex in the graph
port parameter must be a non-empty string
postconditions: edges_from returns Vec of (target_node, connection) tuples
parallel_branches returns Vec of node index vectors, one per branch
invariants: All returned edges have matching source_port
parallel_branches groups edges by extracting branch_N from port name

phase_1: Implement edges_from() using edges_directed and filter
Implement parallel_branches() grouping by branch_N pattern
Write edge case tests for branch parsing

key_sections_to_understand: parallel_branches method signature and grouping logic

title: dag: Add serde serialization tests and integration
description: Add comprehensive serialization tests for WorkflowDAG, verify JSON and MessagePack roundtrip, ensure serde attributes are correct

ubiquitous: THE SYSTEM SHALL serialize WorkflowDag to JSON and MessagePack
THE SYSTEM SHALL deserialize WorkflowDag from JSON and MessagePack

event_driven.shall: THE SYSTEM SHALL serialize all nodes and connections correctly

unwanted.shall_not: THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT panic, but return an error
unwanted.because: Storage failures should not crash the engine

preconditions: WorkflowDag must be in a valid state before serialization
postconditions: Serialized bytes can be deserialized back to equal WorkflowDag
Roundtrip preserves all node indices, names, types, and connections
invariants: Serialization format version must be tracked for migration

phase_1: Verify all types have correct serde attributes

key_sections_to_understand: Existing struct definitions with serde derives
